sections.paragraph:
ὁρατός, ή, όν (ὁράω; Hippocr., Pla. et al.; PGrenf I, 47, 14; LXX; TestReub 4:10; 6:12; Just., D. 3, 7; Tat.) verbal adj. of ὁράω pert. to capability of being seen, visible (w. ἀόρατος; cp. TestReub 6:12; Philo, Op. M. 12, Migr. Abr. 183 ἀόρατος ὡς ἂν ὁρ. ὤν; Ar. 13, 8; Tat. 5, 1) σῶμα Dg 6:4. ἄρχοντες ISm 6:1. Of Christ ὁ ἀόρατος, ὁ διʼ ἡμᾶς ὁρ. IPol 3:2. τὰ ὁρατά (Ps.-Clem., Hom. 2, 7): τὰ ὁρ. καὶ τὰ ἀόρατα things visible and invisible Col 1:16; cp. ITr 5:2; IRo 5:3.—DELG s.v. ὁράω. M-M. TW.

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PGrenf I PGrenf I = BGrenfell, An Alexandrian Erotic Fragment, etc.—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestReub TestReub = Testament of Reuben, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

adj. adj. = adjective

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 719
